reasoning: The threshold to resolve positively is 4 or more 'Protests'. The 360-day average of 3.917 (about 47 per year) reflects a solid background rate of peaceful assembly in Guyana, as massive unrest in April 2025 was likely categorized under 'Riots'. Assuming a Poisson average of 3.9, a random month has a ~55% chance of seeing 4+ events. For the first window (Jan 23 to Feb 21, 2026), multiple distinct protests occurred: the WIN picket in Region 10 (scheduled for 3 days from Jan 28-30) and the Sophia squatters' protest in Georgetown (reported as 2 days on Feb 5-6). Since ACLED codes events daily by location, these are extremely likely to sum to 4 or more events, leaving us confident the first window will surpass the 3.917 threshold. For the second window (Feb 15 to Mar 17, 2026), the forecast date aligns with the start of the window. Relying on the base rate and the somewhat elevated political tensions regarding the 2026 budget and geopolitical shocks, the probability is set around the historical mean distribution of ~55%.
